title: Workspace, Planning, and Memory structure and documentation review
goal: Review whether the workspace, planning, and memory module structures and docs give agents a clear, low-prose operating path.
scope: Root workspace package docs and installed `.agentic-workspace` surfaces
Planning package naming, docs, skills, and closeout surfaces
Memory package naming, docs, skills, and routing surfaces
non_goals: Audit every generated schema page field-by-field

review_mode.review question: Do module docs, names, and skill surfaces make the correct workflow obvious without forcing agents through broad prose?
review_mode.default finding cap: 3 findings; exceeded to capture cross-module naming and closeout drift separately
review_mode.inputs inspected first: `.agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md`, `agentic-workspace skills --target . --task ... --format json`, package READMEs, installed surface docs, module pyprojects, module CLIs

review_method.commands used: agentic-workspace summary; agentic-workspace skills; rg for bootstrap/skills/archive/doc surfaces; targeted Get-Content reads with line numbers
review_method.evidence sources: Static inspection plus friction-confirmed context from recent agent confusion around bootstrap naming, planning creation, and archive/closeout behavior

references.label: Planning public package and script names
Memory public package and script names

findings.title: The workspace layer has no first-party skill surface for its own ordinary workflow
findings.summary: The installed workspace first route is mandatory and CLI-first, but workspace-owned repeatable procedures are not exposed as skills. Skill discovery only knows planning, memory, repo-memory, and repo-tools registries.
findings.evidence: `.agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md` tells agents to use `skills` before prose and to use package skills when recommended. `src/agentic_workspace/cli.py` registers `.agentic-workspace/planning/skills`, `.agentic-workspace/memory/skills`, `.agentic-workspace/memory/repo/skills`, and `tools/skills`, but no `.agentic-workspace/skills` or root workspace skill package. `docs/package/modules.md` defines `routing-only` as having no modules, which also means no module-owned skill path.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents in routing-only or first-contact workspace flows remain dependent on prose fallback for the highest-leverage orientation steps. This weakens the package's own skill-first and compact-output story.
findings.suggested action: Add a small workspace-owned core skill pack or equivalent agent-aid surface for startup orientation, config-obligation interpretation, proof/closeout routing, and lifecycle/update safety. Register it in `agentic-workspace skills` separately from Planning and Memory.
findings.promotion trigger: When optimizing first-contact, routing-only, or cross-module workflow ergonomics

findings.title: `-bootstrap` is still the public and internal identity for non-bootstrap lifecycle modules
findings.summary: Both module packages and public commands still use bootstrap naming even when the commands own ongoing lifecycle, reporting, planning mutation, and memory routing work.
findings.evidence: `packages/planning/pyproject.toml` publishes `agentic-planning-bootstrap`; its CLI help explicitly says the name is historical while it owns `new-plan`, `promote-to-plan`, and `archive-plan`. `packages/memory/pyproject.toml` publishes `agentic-memory-bootstrap`; its CLI includes route, report, sync, promotion, and create-note operations. `docs/planning-cli-naming.md` acknowledges the mismatch but leaves preferred names deferred.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents keep inferring that the CLIs are install-only bootstrap tools or that ongoing workflow should be treated as bootstrap work. That has already produced real workflow confusion.
findings.suggested action: Introduce preferred aliases such as `agentic-planning` and `agentic-memory`, keep `*-bootstrap` as compatibility aliases, and update normal docs, WORKFLOW, command maps, and help to prefer the non-bootstrap names. Defer package/module renames until aliases prove stable.
findings.promotion trigger: Before the next public-facing docs or CLI ergonomics tranche

findings.title: Planning closeout docs still teach archive as the normal completed-plan destination
findings.summary: The implementation now removes completed execplans after closeout distillation by default, but several docs and command names still center archive language.
findings.evidence: `.agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/README.md` says to move completed plans into `execplans/archive/` and says durable residue can use `evidence_only` when the archived plan is the right owner. `packages/planning/README.md` still lists `archive-plan` as the normal command and describes archive output as inactive residue, while the CLI now has `--retain-archive` as a legacy escape hatch.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can continue growing archive files or preserve plan history in Planning when the intended closeout behavior is distill, route durable learning to Memory/docs/contracts/checks/planning, then remove the completed execplan.
findings.suggested action: Rename or alias the normal operation as `close-plan` or `closeout-plan`, make archive retention explicitly legacy/audit-only in the first docs agents see, and update execplan README layout rules to match live-only Planning.
findings.promotion trigger: Before further closeout or archive-retention work

findings.title: The module docs have a good hierarchy in source docs, but the installed workspace still lacks a compact module-level abstraction ladder
findings.summary: Root docs now explain the product, packages, modules, installed surfaces, and generated references well. The installed `.agentic-workspace` layer still presents many deep contract docs and module roots without a tiny skill-backed or structured module map that answers what to open next.
findings.evidence: `docs/index.md`, `docs/package/overview.md`, `docs/package/modules.md`, and `docs/package/installed-surfaces.md` form a coherent source-doc hierarchy. In the installed repo, `.agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md` tells agents to open raw planning, memory, module, or workflow files only when compact output points there, while `.agentic-workspace/docs/` contains many contract pages and no root workspace skill to route the next step.
findings.risk if unchanged: The repo source docs are navigable for maintainers, but installed host-repo agents can still fall back to prose archaeology across `.agentic-workspace/docs`, Planning, and Memory when compact output is insufficient or missed.
findings.suggested action: Add a compact installed module map or workspace skill that exposes the abstraction ladder: workspace orchestration, Planning active execution, Memory durable knowledge, docs/contracts generated reference. Keep it as a router, not another manual.
findings.promotion trigger: When improving installed-host ergonomics or reducing token use

recommendation.promote: Promote the workspace skill surface gap, bootstrap alias migration, and closeout/archive wording drift as implementation issues.
recommendation.defer: Defer a larger docs-IA rewrite; the source documentation hierarchy is already materially better than the installed workflow affordance gap.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not treat Memory's prose/skill structure as a primary bug; Memory has the clearest skill and routing model of the three modules.

retention.trigger: After promoted issues land or are explicitly deferred
retention.proof surface: Issue links plus updated command maps, module docs, skill registries, and closeout docs
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "review workspace planning memory module documentation and structure" --format json
rg -n "bootstrap|agentic-planning-bootstrap|agentic-memory-bootstrap|repo_planning_bootstrap|repo_memory_bootstrap|-bootstrap" pyproject.toml packages src docs README.md .agentic-workspace
drift_log: 2026-05-05: Review created from requested structure/documentation review; no activation occurred.
